entities.name: WHEAT
entities.description: A cereal crop grown in Ethiopia used to study yield gaps and nutrient limitations

entities.name: ETHIOPIA
entities.description: The country where the research on wheat yield gaps was conducted

entities.name: GRAIN YIELD
entities.description: The measure of wheat production used to identify yield-limiting nutrients

entities.name: NUTRIENT AVAILABILITY
entities.description: The presence and accessibility of essential nutrients in the soil affecting crop growth

entities.name: SOIL HETEROGENEITY
entities.description: The variation in soil properties across the landscape contributing to yield gaps

entities.name: UNDULATING LANDSCAPE
entities.description: The uneven terrain that influences nutrient distribution and wheat yield

entities.name: CLIMATE
entities.description: Environmental factors contributing to the wheat yield gap in Ethiopia

entities.name: LANDSCAPE POSITION
entities.description: The specific location within a landscape, such as foot slope, affecting nutrient response

entities.name: RAINFALL REGIME
entities.description: The amount and pattern of precipitation influencing wheat yield and nutrient efficiency

entities.name: NITROGEN
entities.description: An essential plant nutrient denoted as N, identified as yield-limiting

entities.name: PHOSPHORUS
entities.description: An essential plant nutrient denoted as P, identified as yield-limiting

entities.name: POTASSIUM
entities.description: An essential plant nutrient denoted as K, found not to be yield-limiting in this study

entities.name: SULFUR
entities.description: An essential plant nutrient denoted as S, found not to be yield-limiting in this study

entities.name: ZINC
entities.description: An essential plant nutrient denoted as Zn, found not to be yield-limiting in this study

entities.name: BORON
entities.description: An essential plant nutrient denoted as B, found not to be yield-limiting in this study

entities.name: NUTRIENT APPLICATION
entities.description: The application of nutrients in blended, compound, or individual forms to evaluate yield response

entities.name: FOOT SLOPE POSITION
entities.description: A specific landscape position where the highest grain yield improvement was observed
